title: Big banks open earnings season — tape stabilizes
tag: SPY, QQQ, DIA, and IWM all closed higher as volatility eased and the major banks kicked off Q2 earnings.

spy.read: Recovered part of yesterday's pullback, still below the 52-week high

qqq.read: Led today's gainers — tech bounced back after yesterday's leading decline

dia.read: Essentially flat — value held its ground rather than extending

iwm.read: Small caps firmed, in line with the broader recovery

sentiment.label: STEADY
sentiment.qualifier: broad recovery, no single catalyst outside bank earnings

weather.volatility: VIX 16.50, down 3.85% on the day — volatility eased back after yesterday's jump
weather.tone: Calmer — a day to confirm the pullback was noise, not a shift

cluster: Leadership lane: QQQ led the recovery; DIA was flat — growth outpaced value today
Key catalyst today: JPMorgan Chase, Bank of America, Goldman Sachs, Wells Fargo, and Citigroup all reported Q2 earnings this morning
Secondary catalyst: Johnson & Johnson, Morgan Stanley, BlackRock, and Cintas report tomorrow (7/15)
Small cap vs large cap: IWM (+0.35%) roughly matched SPY, no clean divergence
Broad participation: positive across all four majors — clean recovery day
Rotation note: money flowed back into tech after yesterday's rotation out
Behavior rule: one green day recovering one red day is not confirmation of a trend either way
account: Mission for today: observe — a clean recovery day, but one day of bank earnings isn't a full read on the quarter
Broker status: IB sandbox scoped, not yet connected; Schwab pending
Elevated event risk: Johnson & Johnson, Morgan Stanley, BlackRock, and Cintas report tomorrow morning — carries into tomorrow's open
checklist: Data flow, broker connections, overnight positions, stop logic: monitoring data-only — no live broker connection yet
Core universe glance: SPY and IWM clear; QQQ clearest gainer, DIA flat
Day-specific catalyst note #1: big bank Q2 earnings reported this morning (JPM, BAC, GS, WFC, C)
Day-specific catalyst note #2: healthcare, banking, and industrial earnings continue tomorrow morning (JNJ, MS, BLK, CTAS)
body_muted: The tape shrugged off yesterday's pullback — the first wave of bank earnings didn't spook anyone.
